fixture.description: A catch block on a payment path swallows the error silently — no log, no rethrow — and returns null, so a failed charge is indistinguishable from any other null result.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report's MAIN section flags that chargeCustomer swallows the caught error in the catch block — it neither logs nor rethrows nor propagates it, and returns null — so a failed charge is silently hidden and indistinguishable from other null paths on a money-handling path. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL, recommending logging / rethrowing / surfacing the failure. FAIL if the swallowed-error / silent-failure risk is not surfaced in the main report.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security agent flags the empty/silent catch that swallows the error
Finding notes failures are hidden on a payment path
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim the happy path is itself buggy
Does NOT treat returning null as acceptable error handling here
